items.headline: U.S. Futures Slide Amid Renewed Geopolitical Tensions, Oil Prices Surge
items.summary: U.S. stock futures fell sharply on Thursday, April 2, 2026, following President Trump's remarks signaling intensified attacks on Iran, which dampened hopes for a quick resolution to the Middle East conflict and sent oil prices soaring. Despite the pre-market downturn, Wall Street's main indexes were poised to record their largest weekly gains in four months. The S&P 500 closed down 1.14% at 6500 points on April 2, 2026, after a 0.7% gain on April 1. Market sentiment reflects a mix of cautious optimism and geopolitical uncertainty.

items.statContext: Futures slid after President Trump's comments on Iran.
Dow E-minis dropped in pre-market trading.

items.headline: HCL Tech Leads Gains in IT Sector
items.summary: HCL Technologies saw significant gains, benefiting from value buying in IT stocks.

items.headline: Latent View Analytics Surges on AI Investment
items.summary: Shares of Latent View Analytics jumped on the NSE after the company completed a $3M investment in Healtheon AI, a company focused on Agentic AI in healthcare.

items.headline: U Power Soars with Dramatic Increase
items.summary: U Power (UCAR) experienced an exceptionally large percentage gain.

items.headline: Eicher Motors Among Top Losers
items.summary: Eicher Motors was one of the biggest decliners today.

items.headline: Asian Paints Records Decline
items.summary: Asian Paints shares fell, contributing to the day's losses.

items.headline: Nike Drops on Weak Outlook
items.summary: Nike's stock sank by more than 15% following a weak outlook.

items.headline: Fed Officials Bowman and Logan to Deliver Key Speeches on Interest Rates
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman and Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan are scheduled to deliver remarks today, April 2, 2026, which are closely watched by investors for clarity on the future interest rate path. The federal funds rate remains steady at 3.50% to 3.75%. Bowman is expected to emphasize proactive economic measures, while Logan will address the impact of surging oil prices.

items.headline: U.S. Goods and Services Deficit Rises in February
items.summary: The U.S. goods and services deficit increased to $57.3 billion in February, up $2.7 billion from January. Exports rose by 4.2% to $314.8 billion, and imports increased by 4.3% to $372.1 billion.

items.headline: Small Business Sales See Steady Growth in March Despite Cautious Spending
items.summary: U.S. small business sales grew steadily in March 2026, with a 1.3% year-over-year increase, primarily driven by higher average ticket sizes (+2.6%). While transaction volumes slowed, the sector closed Q1 on solid footing. Gasoline station sales saw a notable jump due to rising energy prices.

items.headline: Acuity Brands Beats Q2 Earnings Estimates
items.summary: Acuity Brands ($AYI) reported strong Q2 earnings of $4.14 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $4.01 per share by 3.37%. This performance compares favorably to $3.73 per share a year ago.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads as Investors Rotate into Value and Small-Caps
items.summary: Energy stocks are leading among equity sectors, with the MSCI AC World Energy index rising 35.7% over the past year due to surging oil and gas prices. In Q1 2026, investors showed a notable rotation into value stocks, small-caps, and dividend payers, while technology and communication services lagged.
